title: THE UNSEEN CONTROLLER | Bhagavad Gita Chapter 9 Verses 7 - 10 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Okay, so the topic today is Unseen Controller, we are covering 3 verses from the 9th chapter
verse number 7 to 10, we will chant this verse together, please repeat after me, Sarvabhutani
Kaunteya, Prakriti Myanti Mamikam, Kalpakshai Punastani, Kalpada Vishjamiham, Kalpada Vishjamiham,
Kalpada Vishjamiham,
O son of Kunti, at the end of millennium, every material manifestation enters into my nature and at the beginning of another millennium, by my potency, I again create.
So these are the words spoken by Lord Krishna to Arjuna, he says that the end of millennium, what is the meaning of millennium?
Thousand years of Brahma life is one answer, one yuga is one millennium, anybody else?
What is one millennium?
So one millennium is, yes Vikas, okay, one millennium is one life of Brahma, Lord Brahma,
lives for hundred years and to give you some idea about the duration of life of Lord Brahma,
one day of Lord Brahma is called one kalpa and one kalpa is the combination of thousand cycles of four yugas,
what are the four yugas?
Satya Yuga, Treta Yuga, Dwapa Yuga and Kali Yuga.
So the four yugas combined together form,
One Mahayuga and thousand Mahayugas form one Kalpa, sorry one Kalpa and similar is the duration of the night of Lord Brahma and like that one month, one year and hundred years.
So the Mahapralaya where Krishna says this creation, this universe is created and then annihilated, it's at the end of millennium.
Who is the first created being of the universe? Lord Brahma.
So first created being is Brahma and this knowledge about this creation was imparted to Lord Brahma.
Tene Brahma Hridaye Adi Kavaye.
So this Vedic knowledge is not the Bhagavad Gita, Mahabharata, Ramayana, Mahabharata, Supanishad, Itihasas.
All this, these are all Vedic knowledge.
And this knowledge is also called Apogea.
It is called Krushaya because it is not man-made.
Just like we have an understanding that whenever anybody produces a gadget, whatever we buy in the market, it comes with the manual.

And the manual is made because the manufacturer knows if the product has to be optimally, efficiently used, the user has to be guided, has to be told what this product is all about, the do's and don'ts, how to use it effectively.
So likewise this creation, this gigantic creation, there is an intelligent creator.
There is lot of orders.
The sun rises in the morning, the seasons change, it rains.
There is an intelligent creator. It has not happened by chance.
It is not an accidental creation.
So we will come to that in today's session.
Why we say it is not an accidental creation.
It is an intelligent creator behind it.
So he created this universe.
The first created being Brahma was imparted this knowledge about this creation.
And Tene Brahma Hridaye.
And this knowledge was coming down and it is also called Shruti.
Shruti means through aural reception.
And this Vedavyasa, who is the literary incarnation of God, put this knowledge into writing and further subdivided into four parts.
And that is how we have the four Vedas.
Veda, Samveda, Yajurveda and Atharva Veda.
So thousand cycles of four yugas form one day of Brahma.
One day of Brahma is also called one Kalpa.
And like that, one lifetime of Brahma, which is hundred years.
At the end of that, the dissolution or annihilation of the universe happens.
Text 8.
The whole cosmic order is under me.
By my will, it is manifested again and again.
And by my will, it is annihilated at the end.
See Lord Krishna is saying these words.
We have different Devi and Devatas being worshipped in India specifically.
But nowhere you will find somebody very boldly declaring this whole creation has come from me.
Under my will it is being manifested.
It is being created and annihilated.
So here Lord is very boldly declaring, by my will it is manifested again and again.
So the creation comes about, stays for some time, it is maintained and it is annihilated.
So this material world which we are part of now, it is temporary in the sense, it is created at a time.
It remains for some time and then it gets annihilated.
Unlike this material world, there is a spiritual world which Krishna talks about in the Gita.
Parastasma tu bhavanyo vyakta avyakta sanatana.
This world which we are part of is called vyakta.
Vyakta means manifest.
Vyakta.
Avyakta means unmanifest.
It is manifest for a while.
And then it becomes unmanifest.
It is temporary.
Parastasma tu bhavanyo.
Beyond this material world, there is a spiritual world which is not vyakta and avyakta.
And that's what the Lord is inviting us to come back.
And by my will it is annihilated at the end.
Now here, the Lord is very boldly saying, whole cosmic order is under me.
In present times, only people like Nirav Modi can make a statement.
The entire banking system and the funds are under me.
Now, let's take one example.
Krishna is saying that yes, this whole cosmic creation, it has come about by my will.
It remains for some time by my will and then eventually it gets destroyed.
Let's take one example.
Today we are living in a world where

That is also called Maha Vishnu or Karunadakshaya Vishnu.
In each universe, the Lord further expands as Garbhodakshaya Vishnu.
And from the navel of this Garbhodakshaya, Garbo means the Garbha, and from the navel of the Garbhodakshaya Vishnu comes the lotus flower,
the stem of which has the 14 planetary systems and of the lotus flower is Lord Brahma.
And in that particular universe, the Lord further expands as Shirodakshaya Vishnu, which is also called the Supersoul or Paramatma,
which is present in each and every atom, which is present in each and every living being.

The Lord creates these material universes through Maha Vishnu.

And the first created being is Lord Brahma.

which comprises each day's one Kalpa, which is equivalent to one Maha Yuga, which is 1000 cycles of 4 Yugas.

and then survival of the fittest, and then we evolved.

building blocks of life, amino acids and all that came together by some arrangement.

resulted in a very, very primitive life form.

And then, with passage of time, survival of the fittest,
they adapted to the changes which were happening in the surroundings.

slowly, this unit of animals became more and more complex.
and then eventually the most developed life form is man.

life came into existence and slowly it evolved.

Do you understand that? There is a difference.
The house, this house has not changed, the person has transmigrated from one house to another.
So what Vedas are saying is, there are 84 lakh species of life ever existing.
They are like, in Mumbai city, slums are also there, 1 BHK house is there, 2 BHK also is there.
But the soul is moving from one form of life to another life.
Do you understand?
Yes.

If for some reason my financial situation deteriorates, I move to a smaller house.
So the Vedic understanding is, all these species of life are existing.
They are living from the dawn of creation.

After arriving in the United States in 1965 at the age of 69,
Srila Prabhupada formed the International Society for Krishna Consciousness,
which developed into a worldwide spiritual community
with hundreds of ashrams, schools, temples and farms,
and with thousands of dedicated disciples
who made Hare Krishna a household word.
To have achieved all this in only 12 years was remarkable.
But before he passed away from this world in 1977,
Srila Prabhupada had also written and published
over 80 volumes of translation and commentary
on the great spiritual classics of ancient India.
His books were welcomed with great critical acclaim
by the Western academic community
and by scholars from India as well,
and they became standard texts in almost every university
and library around the world.
Srila Prabhupada had also succeeded in transmitting
the profound wisdom of the ancient Vedic culture
in a form and style that was clearly
understandable to the average modern man.
He could somehow explain even the most difficult philosophical concepts
to people who were totally unfamiliar
with the complex Indian religious tradition.
Srila Prabhupada circled the globe 11 times
between 1966 and 1977,
and everywhere he went,
he taught the same basic truths
and fundamental principles of religion
that constitute the cornerstone
of spiritual understanding for everyone,
no matter what their nationality or religion.
Srila Prabhupada was, more than anything, a teacher,
a professor emeritus of the science of self-realization,
and he was the best kind of teacher
because he always lived up to what he taught.

fficially delayed his half-life,

will continue to Hail Mahāprabha.

A wonderful machine, it is taking the picture and it will move. But there is an operator. Where is the machine which is working without operator? Can you give any example? Here is a machine which is working without operator.

So how do you think that the nature machine is working without the Supreme Operator, God's instruction? How do you think it? This is not very reasonable. We have to judge. There are different evidences. One of the evidence is hypothesis.
That hypothesis is that because we see that no one is working without the Supreme Operator, we are not able to judge.

No machine works without operator. Therefore we should conclude it. Even though we do not know what is God, what is the nature, we must conclude it that the nature which is working under some Supreme Operator, that is God.
It is not necessary to see the operator, but we can guess that there must be operator.
So human life is...
It is meant for finding out who is the true operator. That is human life. Otherwise it's cats and dogs like. They are eating, sleeping, mating and dancing. That's all. That is not human life.
You must find out who is the operator. This is called in Sanskrit word. Now this human form of life is meant for inquiring about the Supreme Operator.
Now that Supreme Operator, Krishna, is so kind. He is giving evidence in the Bhagavad Gita.
Now here I am. Under my direction, the prakriti, the nature, material nature is work.
So this verse which we just now talked about is the next verse in the same series of verses.
So as we started our conversation, this is the verse.
So this is the 10th verse.
Here the Lord is saying, this material nature is working under my direction.
We talked about the satellites. The scientists can say that yes, we are controlling the satellites.
So who is controlling all these galaxies, these stars, these planets moving in their respective orbits?
Krishna is saying, this nature is working under my direction.
O son of Kunti! And it is producing all moving and unmoving beings.
By its rule, this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again.
Now I will give you another example. What is this picture? Anyone knows the name?
Loudly everyone.
Mona Lisa.
Who is the painter?
So we see a beautiful piece of art, we know that there is an artist.
We see a landscape, a nice beautiful landscape painted by someone, we know that there is an artist behind it.
Somebody has put the brushes together, the colours together and using his artistic skills has put this nice piece of art.
Another piece of landscape, we know there is an artist.
Likewise in natures around us, we see beautiful pieces of art, like the flowers.
Unlike Mona Lisa painting, it is a two dimensional painting, you see beautiful flowers, very aromatic, colourful, fragrant, three dimensional, you can feel it.
You see beautiful birds, so much of symmetry.
The colours.
Beautiful butterflies.
So when we see a beautiful piece of art like Mona Lisa or a painting, it is very very clear in our mind that there is an artist.
There is somebody who has brought this piece of art into creation, into existence.
So likewise when we see beautiful pieces of art in this creation around us, which is in abundance, we should ask ourselves, who is the artist?
So here in this verse, Krishna is saying, who is the artist?
Who is the artist?
So here in this verse, Krishna is saying, under my direction, prakriti suyate sa cara acharam, moving and non-moving beings.
So tree, as mentioned, is a non-moving being.
Flower, all this is a non-moving being.
And the supreme director is the Lord.
He is the background of all the material manifestation.
All the living entities in different forms and species, Krishna says, aham bija pradapita, I am the seed giving father.
Different bodies, they get according to their past desires and their deeds.
Based on our inclination, based on our unfulfilled desires, life after life, the soul gets different bodies and moves from one form to another.
Material nature without the superintendence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead cannot do anything.
Just like these days we have driverless cars, you know, Google has come up with cars and all that.
Is it really driverless?
Yes? No?
Is it moving on its own?
No.
Just like we remotely control a projector or an AC or something like that.
It's a technology that instead of we physically pressing the button, I have something, a gadget whereby I can control that gadget through some,
but still there is an operator.
Even driverless cars, there is somebody who is controlling the monitoring of those cars, maybe through other infrared rays or whatever it is.
So there is an operator.
So Krishna here is saying, without,
this whole creation, this material nature is working under my direction.
Yes, the Supreme Personality is detached from, although this creation is there, it's moving, he is neutral.
